message:
perf(sse): add event type string interning and zero-copy fast path
for SSE stream parsing to eliminate per-event allocations

intern_event_type():
- Map common SSE event names (message, message_start, message_stop,
  message_delta, content_block_start/delta/stop, ping, error) to
  Cow::Borrowed static strings, eliminating one String allocation per
  SSE event on the hot streaming path — only unknown event types
  fall through to Cow::Owned

Zero-copy fast path in feed_into():
- Extract core line-processing logic into standalone process_source()
  function that operates directly on &str slices with explicit
  bom_checked/current/has_data state parameters
- When self.buffer is empty (common case after complete chunk delivery),
  process data in-place without copying to buffer, only buffering the
  unconsumed tail (incomplete line fragment at end of chunk)
- When buffer already has content (partial line from previous feed),
  fall back to existing append + process + drain path
- Reduces one unnecessary String::push_str + mem::take per complete
  SSE chunk delivery

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
